name: Shirdi
description: Shirdi is a magnificent destination in Maharashtra, India. Known for its remarkable blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Visitors flock here to experience its unique atmosphere, vibrant local markets, and iconic landmarks. Whether you are seeking spiritual peace, thrilling adventures, or simply a relaxing holiday, Shirdi offers a memorable experience that captures the true essence of Indian heritage. The city's charm lies in its timeless appeal and warm hospitality.
history: Shirdi boasts a rich history spanning several centuries, serving as a prominent center for trade, culture, and power under various dynasties.

topAttractions.name: Samadhi Mandir (Main Shrine)
topAttractions.description: The white marble shrine built over the tomb (samadhi) of Sai Baba of Shirdi (died 1918). A life-size white Italian marble statue of Sai Baba sits in the sanctum. The pilgrims' queue (darshan line) can be 2-8 hours. VIP darshan passes available (₹300-500). The Sai Baba Sansthan Trust serves 50,000+ pilgrims daily.

topAttractions.name: Dwarkamai Mosque
topAttractions.description: The mosque where Sai Baba lived for 60 years until his death in 1918. A fire (dhuni) has been burning here continuously since Sai Baba lit it. His personal items — grinding stone, iron pot, wooden plank he slept on — are preserved here. The sacred mosque accepts all religions. Entry: Free.

topAttractions.name: Chavadi
topAttractions.description: A small hall where Sai Baba slept every alternate night. His palanquin is preserved here and taken out in procession on Thursday evenings. One of the key pilgrimage stops.

topAttractions.name: Lendi Baug (Garden)
topAttractions.description: A garden planted by Sai Baba himself where he daily watered the plants and tended the trees. He would also meditate under a Neem tree here. Now a maintained garden with the lamp (deep) that Sai Baba kept burning and a small temple.

topAttractions.name: Shri Saibaba Museum
topAttractions.description: A museum containing Sai Baba's personal belongings — his satka (staff), blanket, books, and photographs. The museum traces his life from arriving in Shirdi as a young man to his Mahasamadhi in 1918.

topAttractions.name: Khandoba Temple
topAttractions.description: The temple of Khandoba (a manifestation of Shiva) on the edge of Shirdi where Sai Baba first arrived as a young man. The priest Mhalsapati greeted him with "Ya Sai!" (Welcome, Saint!) — the origin of the name "Sai Baba". An important first stop for pilgrims.

culture: The culture of Shirdi is a vibrant tapestry of traditional arts, festivals, and culinary delights, deeply influenced by its historical roots.
travelTips: Book accommodations in advance during peak season
Try the local street food but ensure it is hot and fresh
Use authorized taxis or rideshare apps
Respect local customs and dress modestly at religious sites

itineraries.name: Highlights Tour
activities: Day 1: Arrival, City Center Attractions, Evening Market
Day 2: Historical Sites, Local Food Tasting, Departure
